tasks.task: Verify existing auth infrastructure (AC: #1) Review authService.ts for existing guard functions Confirm withAdmin() wrapper pattern in resolvers.ts Identify any gaps in current role enforcement

Create centralized policy module (AC: #2) Create src/service/auth/rbacPolicy.ts Define PolicyName type/enum for named policies Create policy registry mapping names to role requirements Export getPolicy(name: PolicyName) function

Add league-scoped access guard (AC: #3) Implement assertLeagueAccess(ctx, leagueId) in authService.ts ADMIN bypasses league check (can access all leagues) MANAGER must have a player record in the league PLAYER (viewer) can access all leagues (read-only) Log scope violations with userId and requestedLeagueId

Add season-scoped access guard (AC: #3) Implement assertSeasonAccess(ctx, seasonId) in authService.ts Derive leagueId from seasonId for validation Apply same logic as league scoping Log scope violations with userId, seasonId, and leagueId

Create withPolicy() higher-order function (AC: #1, #2) Implement withPolicy(policyName, resolverFn) wrapper Support multiple policies: withPolicy(['admin', 'managerOfTeam'], resolverFn) Integrate with existing logging for audit trail Return proper GraphQL errors with codes
Migrate withAdmin() to policy system (AC: #2) Refactor withAdmin() to use withPolicy('admin', ...) Ensure backward compatibility with existing resolvers Verify all 27 mutations still work correctly

Write comprehensive unit tests (AC: #4) Create test/service/auth/rbacPolicy.test.ts Test: Admin can access any resource Test: Manager can only access scoped resources Test: Player (viewer) has read-only access Test: Unauthenticated users are denied (401) Test: League scoping enforced correctly Test: Season scoping enforced correctly Test: Policy changes take effect immediately

criterion.given: an API handler or GraphQL resolver
criterion.when: a policy helper is applied with required role
criterion.then: unauthorized roles receive HTTP 403 Forbidden AND authorized roles proceed to the handler logic
the updated policy is reflected immediately AND no code duplication exists across endpoints

criterion.given: a policy helper with league/season scoping
criterion.when: a user attempts to access resources outside their scope
criterion.then: access is denied with 403 AND the denial is logged with user context
all RBAC scenarios pass (admin access, manager scoped, viewer read-only, unauthorized denial)

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: RBAC: admin/manager/viewer enforced at handler/resolver entry; deny by default; policies encapsulated per feature. All queries/writes scoped by league/season/team.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: NextAuth sessions with RBAC (admin/manager/viewer) enforced at API layer; session-backed via secure cookies. Role checks at handler/resolver entry.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Create reusable policy helpers for APIs to consistently enforce roles. League/season scoping for access control. Unit tests for all RBAC scenarios.

doc.title: PRD
doc.snippet: FR-016: Admins have full write access. FR-017: Team Managers have limited write access (scoped to their team). FR-018: Viewers have read-only access.

file.symbol: assertRole, logAuthCheck, AuthError, requireRole, requireAdmin, requireAtLeast, assertManagerOfTeam
file.reason: Core auth guards to extend with league/season scoping. Contains existing RBAC implementation patterns.
Existing withAdmin() wrapper to migrate to new policy system. All 27 mutations use this pattern.
Context types used by auth guards. ServerContext includes prisma client.
Data model for league/season scoping. Player.userId links users to seasons via teams.

file.symbol: assertRole, requireRole, requireAdmin, requireAtLeast, assertManagerOfTeam tests
file.reason: Test patterns to follow. Uses createCtx(), makeUser() from test utilities.
Integration test patterns with real database. Tests all role combinations.
Test context creation utility. Provides prisma mock and user context.

file.symbol: makeUser, insertUser, insertLeague, insertSeason, insertTeam, insertPlayer
file.reason: Test data factories for creating users, leagues, seasons, teams, players.

node.package: Functional programming library with Option type for user presence checks
Authentication library (stay on v4, do not upgrade to v5)

constraints.constraint: Deny by default - guards at resolver entry point
Role enforcement via API layer, not middleware
All queries/writes scoped by league/season/team per FR-020
Use Effect Option for user presence checks
Structured JSON logging with requestId correlation
GraphQL errors with extensions.code for HTTP status
Do NOT change existing assertRole() or assertManagerOfTeam() signatures
Maintain backward compatibility with existing withAdmin() usage

interface.name: assertRole
interface.signature: assertRole(ctx: AuthContext, allowed: Role[], endpoint?: string): AuthContext

interface.name: assertManagerOfTeam
interface.signature: assertManagerOfTeam(ctx: ServerContext, teamId: string, endpoint?: string): Promise<ServerContext>

interface.name: logAuthCheck
interface.signature: logAuthCheck(requestId: string, outcome: "allow" | "deny" | "unauthorized", details: LogAuthCheckDetails): void

interface.name: AuthError
interface.signature: class AuthError extends Error { constructor(message: string, status: 401 | 403); }

tests.standards: Jest 30.2.0 with @swc/jest for fast TypeScript compilation. Tests mirror src/ structure in test/ directory. Use createCtx() from test/utils.ts for context creation with prisma mock. Use makeUser(), insertUser(), insertLeague(), etc. from test/modelFactory.ts for test data. Integration tests use real SQLite test database via Prisma mock. Run tests with: npm test -- --runInBand Mock next-auth/next with jest.mock for unit tests.

ideas.idea: Test withPolicy('admin') allows ADMIN, denies MANAGER/PLAYER
Test withPolicy('manager') allows ADMIN and MANAGER, denies PLAYER
Test getPolicy() returns correct config for each PolicyName
Test policy registry has no duplicate entries
Test assertLeagueAccess allows ADMIN for any league
Test assertLeagueAccess allows PLAYER (viewer) for any league (read-only)
Test assertLeagueAccess allows MANAGER only for their league
Test assertLeagueAccess denies MANAGER outside their league with 403
Test assertSeasonAccess follows same pattern as league
Test scope violations are logged with userId, requestedId
Integration test: all 27 mutations still work with migrated withAdmin
